signals.idea:
Refactor edit mode ownership out of useInpainting

## Problem Statement
The edit mode concept has grown to 6+ modes (text, inpaint, annotate, reposition, img2img, upscale, enhance), but useInpainting — a domain-specific hook for paint/annotate/text — still acts as the source of truth for editMode. This creates:

1. Three competing type definitions for the same concept:
   - src/domains/media-lightbox/hooks/inpainting/types.ts: EditMode = text | inpaint | annotate (narrow)
   - src/domains/media-lightbox/model/editSettingsTypes.ts: EditMode = text | inpaint | annotate | reposition | img2img | enhance (wide)
   - src/domains/media-lightbox/contexts/ImageEditCanvasContext.tsx: ImageEditMode = inpaint | annotate | reposition | img2img | text | upscale | null (wider still)

2. Lossy round-trip: toInpaintingEditMode() in both useImageEditOrchestrator.ts and useInlineEditState.ts narrows reposition/img2img/enhance to text when passing to the inpainting hook. Then as ImageEditState setEditMode casts to widen on the way back up. We just patched a bug where the read path was losing the mode.

3. Inverted ownership: The orchestrator defers to inpainting.editMode for state it should own directly. Reposition, img2img, and upscale are peers of inpainting — not children of it.

## Goal
- Single canonical EditMode type used everywhere
- Edit mode state owned at the orchestrator/persistence level, not by useInpainting
- useInpainting receives only what it needs (whether its active, and which sub-mode if active) without owning the broader concept
- Remove toInpaintingEditMode() bridge functions and type casts
- No behavioral changes — all modes should continue to work exactly as they do now

## Key Files
- src/domains/media-lightbox/hooks/inpainting/types.ts — narrow EditMode type
- src/domains/media-lightbox/model/editSettingsTypes.ts — wide EditMode type (should be canonical)
- src/domains/media-lightbox/contexts/ImageEditCanvasContext.tsx — ImageEditMode type
- src/domains/media-lightbox/contexts/ImageEditContext.tsx — ImageEditState interface
- src/domains/media-lightbox/hooks/useInpainting.ts — currently owns editMode state
- src/domains/media-lightbox/hooks/useImageEditOrchestrator.ts — should own editMode, has toInpaintingEditMode
- src/domains/media-lightbox/model/buildImageEditStateValue.ts — builds context value, just patched to use explicit editMode param
- src/tools/edit-images/hooks/useInlineEditState.ts — inline editor path, also has toInpaintingEditMode
- src/domains/media-lightbox/hooks/useEditModePanelState.ts — consumes editMode from context
- src/domains/media-lightbox/components/EditModePanel.tsx — UI consumer
- src/domains/media-lightbox/hooks/persistence/useEditSettingsPersistence.ts — persists editMode
- src/domains/media-lightbox/hooks/useGenerationEditSettings.ts — settings layer with editMode
- src/domains/media-lightbox/model/buildImageEditStateValue.test.ts — existing tests

unresolved_flags.concern: Image edit mode naming: the plan does not resolve the repository's `enhance` vs `upscale` split or define how persisted last-used values are normalized.
Edit-mode ownership: keeping `useInpainting` fallback edit-mode state leaves the source-of-truth inversion in place, so the refactor would only partially meet its stated goal.

Verification: the plan relies on broad suite runs but omits targeted tests for the two bridge-removal points (`useImageEditOrchestrator`, `useInlineEditState`), where the reported mode-loss bug actually occurs.

Did the work fully address the issue hints, user notes, and approved plan requirements?: The plan does not fully satisfy its own ownership goal. It explicitly keeps `useInpainting` fallback edit-mode state (`src/domains/media-lightbox/hooks/useInpainting.ts:74-112`) even though the stated goal/success criteria say edit mode should be owned at the orchestrator/persistence layer. In this repo, the only non-test callers already pass controlled edit-mode props (`src/domains/media-lightbox/hooks/useImageEditOrchestrator.ts:190-214`, `src/tools/edit-images/hooks/useInlineEditState.ts:175-197`), so leaving the fallback in place preserves the inverted ownership the refactor is supposed to remove.

Did the work fully address the issue hints, user notes, and approved plan requirements?: The plan treats adding `upscale` to `EditMode` as the unification step, but the repo already shows a deeper naming conflict: image UI/context use `upscale` (`src/domains/media-lightbox/contexts/ImageEditCanvasContext.tsx:21`, `src/domains/media-lightbox/hooks/useEditModePanelState.ts:169-173`) while the canonical settings type still uses `enhance` (`src/domains/media-lightbox/model/editSettingsTypes.ts:1`). Without deciding which name is canonical and how persisted values are normalized, the plan does not fully address the approved 'single canonical EditMode' requirement.

Did the work fully address the issue hints, user notes, and approved plan requirements?: Step 7 is partially stale against the repo. `buildImageEditStateValue.test.ts` already has reposition coverage (`src/domains/media-lightbox/model/buildImageEditStateValue.test.ts:170-203`), so adding another reposition pass-through case there is not the main missing coverage. The real untested paths are the orchestrator and inline-editor bridge removals.

Are the proposed changes technically correct?: Keeping both `'enhance'` and `'upscale'` in the canonical image edit union would still leave two representations for one concept. The repo currently uses `upscale` in image-edit UI, while `enhance` is otherwise only present in the settings type/video submode. `useLastUsedEditSettings` blindly merges stored values from localStorage/DB (`src/domains/media-lightbox/hooks/useLastUsedEditSettings.ts:82-129`), so the plan needs an explicit normalization or migration decision instead of just widening the union.

Are the proposed changes technically correct?: The plan's 'just pass the wide type through' argument is incomplete because downstream canvas code still narrows/casts edit mode outside `useInpainting`: `StrokeOverlayDrawingProps.editMode` is a separate union (`src/domains/media-lightbox/components/strokeOverlay/useStrokeOverlayDrawing.ts:12-27`) and `MediaDisplayWithCanvas` casts context edit mode into that union (`src/domains/media-lightbox/components/MediaDisplayWithCanvas.tsx:389-405`). If those files are left untouched, the refactor will not actually remove all edit-mode bridges/casts.

Are the proposed changes technically correct?: The plan assumes keeping `useInpainting`'s uncontrolled fallback is harmless, but that also preserves its broader setter API (`EditMode | updater` in `src/domains/media-lightbox/hooks/useInpainting.ts:106-112`) while orchestrator/persistence setters are plain mode setters. The repo currently avoids updater usage, but the plan does not reconcile this API mismatch.

Is the scope and scale of the change appropriate?: The proposed file list is too narrow for the stated goal 'single canonical EditMode type used everywhere'. The repo still has non-canonical edit-mode typing outside the listed files, including `src/domains/media-lightbox/components/strokeOverlay/useStrokeOverlayDrawing.ts:23`, `src/domains/media-lightbox/components/MediaDisplayWithCanvas.tsx:401`, `src/domains/media-lightbox/components/editModes/GenerateButton.tsx:16`, and `src/domains/media-lightbox/hooks/useRepositionGestureHandlers.ts:6`. The plan should either include these or narrow the goal.

Is the scope and scale of the change appropriate?: Test scope is mismatched to the actual risky behavior. The behavioral change happens where `toInpaintingEditMode` is removed from `useImageEditOrchestrator` and `useInlineEditState`, but the plan only names `buildImageEditStateValue.test.ts` plus broad suite runs. A minimal but sufficient scope should add targeted assertions for those two integration points.

Is there convincing verification for the change?: There is no existing test coverage for the main lightbox bug path: `useImageEditOrchestrator` returning the narrowed inpainting mode instead of the persisted mode. Repository test search shows no orchestrator test exercising `editOrchestrator.editMode` or `ImageLightbox`'s `isRepositionMode` check (`src/domains/media-lightbox/ImageLightbox.tsx:331-336`).

Is there convincing verification for the change?: `useInlineEditState.test.ts` only verifies that `useInpainting` initially receives `editMode: 'text'` (`src/tools/edit-images/hooks/__tests__/useInlineEditState.test.ts:311-325`). It does not cover non-inpainting modes (`reposition`, `img2img`, `upscale`) or the `imageEditValue.setEditMode` wrapper, so removing the inline bridge would not be convincingly verified.

Is there convincing verification for the change?: `buildImageEditStateValue.test.ts` already covers reposition/img2img pass-through, but it also uses an impossible `'magic-edit' as any` fixture (`src/domains/media-lightbox/model/buildImageEditStateValue.test.ts:164-196`). The plan does not clean that up, so one of the central tests remains weaker than it should be for a type-unification refactor.

Is there convincing verification for the change?: `useInpainting.test.ts` only exercises `text` and `annotate` controlled props (`src/domains/media-lightbox/hooks/__tests__/useInpainting.test.ts:63-140`). If `useInpainting` is widened to accept the canonical type, adjacent cases like `reposition`/`img2img`/`upscale` should be asserted explicitly.

signals.loop_summary: Iteration 1. Weighted score trajectory: 24.5. Plan deltas: n/a. Recurring critiques: 0. Resolved flags: 0. Open significant flags: 15.

items.criterion: Only one EditMode type definition exists (in editSettingsTypes.ts), covering all modes
No toInpaintingEditMode function exists anywhere in the codebase
No 'as ImageEditMode', 'as ImageEditState[...]', or 'as any' type casts related to editMode in the touched files
useInpainting accepts the canonical EditMode type (not a narrow subset)
useImageEditOrchestrator returns persistedEditMode (wide type) not inpaintingHook.editMode (narrow type)
npx tsc --noEmit passes with zero errors
All existing tests in src/domains/media-lightbox/ pass
No behavioral changes — all edit modes (text, inpaint, annotate, reposition, img2img, upscale, enhance) continue to work
ImageEditMode type alias is removed or replaced with EditMode | null
useInpainting no longer owns editMode state — it receives it as a required prop from the orchestrator/persistence layer
Each touched file is under ~300 lines
Manual smoke test: switch between all edit modes in lightbox and inline editor
